steps.title: Calculate the mean of each data set
List deviations from each mean
steps.content:
Data set X (deviations from 15):
13 − 15 = −2 (appears twice)
14 − 15 = −1 (appears twice)
15 − 15 = 0 (appears once)
16 − 15 = 1 (appears twice)
17 − 15 = 2 (appears twice)

Data set Y (deviations from 4):
2 − 4 = −2 (appears twice)
3 − 4 = −1 (appears twice)
4 − 4 = 0 (appears once)
5 − 4 = 1 (appears twice)
6 − 4 = 2 (appears twice)

steps.title: Apply the standard deviation concept
steps.content:
Standard deviation measures spread around the mean. It depends only on how far each value deviates from the mean, not on the absolute values. Both data sets have identical deviation patterns: two values at −2, two at −1, one at 0, two at +1, and two at +2. When the deviations are identical, the standard deviations are equal.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
